channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Is Negotiating an Iran Deal That Would Buy Time, Again
item.description: The United States proposed a 20-year “suspension” of all nuclear activity, even as President Trump demands assurances that Iran can never build a nuclear weapon.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance arriving at Joint Base Andrews from Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday. Mr. Vance there were “some good conversations” with Iran in Pakistan, and the ball is now in Tehran’s court.

item.title: Amid War, G.O.P. Puts Off Testimony From Senior Pentagon Commanders
item.description: House lawmakers had been scheduled to hear testimony next week from the head of military operations in the Middle East, but the Republican majority postponed the hearing until late May.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We are six weeks into this conflict, and we still haven’t gotten a public briefing from anyone in the administration about the war,” said Representative Adam Smith of Washington, the ranking Democrat on the Armed Services Committee.

item.title: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
item.description: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A ship in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Sunday.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
item.description: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold, Jill Cowan and Emily Cochrane
item.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California had been in a crowded field vying to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

item.title: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
item.description: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Tony Gonzales during a House Committee on Homeland Security hearing in Washington in February.

item.title: What Happens in California Governor’s Race After Eric Swalwell Stepped Down?
item.description: For Democrats, the drama is reverberating well beyond the state.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Explanation for an Image of Himself as Jesus: ‘I Thought It Was Me as a Doctor’
item.description: The image showed President Trump bathed in divine light and clad in religious robes. His interpretation was that the image depicted him as a doctor, not Jesus Christ.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said on Monday that he had thought the image he had posted to his Truth Social account had depicted him not as Jesus — but as a physician.

item.title: With Algeria Visit, Pope Leo Returns to His Augustinian Roots
item.description: Some theologians say the pontiff’s understanding of Augustine’s teachings helped inform his response to President Trump’s negative comments about him.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.media:description: Pope Leo in Algiers, Algeria, on Monday.

item.title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Stay Out of U.S. Affairs
item.description: The vice president, a Catholic, defended President Trump, who had accused the pope of being too liberal and “weak on crime.” The pontiff has said that he has “no fear of the Trump administration.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Pakistan on Sunday.

item.title: Canada Special Elections 2026 Results: Carney’s Liberal Party Gains Majority
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney, constrained by leading a minority government for the past year, gained a majority in the House of Commons after special elections on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada at the Liberal Party’s convention in Montreal on Saturday. His agenda is focused on reducing Canada’s dependence on the United States.

item.title: He Warned About the Dangers of A.I. If Only His Father Had Listened.
item.description: Ben Riley was already writing about the risks of chatbots when his dad started trusting A.I. over his doctor.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Rosenbluth
item.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Erratic Behavior and Extreme Comments Revive Mental Health Debate
item.description: As the president threatens to wipe out Iran and attacks the pope, even some former allies and advisers are questioning whether he has grown increasingly unbalanced, describing him as “lunatic” and “clearly insane.”
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump in Charlottesville, Va., last week.

item.title: Greg Abbott Threatens to Cut $110 Million From Houston Police Over ICE Policy Dispute
item.description: The office of Gov. Greg Abbott wants the city to stop enforcing a new ordinance governing how its officers engage with ICE.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:description: One of the sponsors of the ordinance accused Gov. Greg Abbott, above, of “attempting to defund the police.”

item.title: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
item.description: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Many of President Trump’s supporters have seen Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary as a kindred spirit, and as an incubator of ideas that they embraced.

item.title: ‘We Finally Have Democracy’: Hungarians Erupt in Joy and Relief
item.description: Many Hungarians, exhausted by a struggling economy and worried about a move away from the European Union, were jubilant after Prime Minister Viktor Orban conceded. Some had wondered if he would.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Lili Rutai
item.media:description: Budapest was lit by torchlight as people gathered to celebrate the Hungarian election results on Sunday.

item.title: New Rules Hinder Foreign Firms From Moving Supply Chains From China
item.description: Multinationals in China are concerned that the regulations could allow authorities to penalize companies and executives for shifting supply chains away from the country.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: Steel pipes await export in Lianyungang, China. The country’s trade surplus reached nearly $1.2 trillion last year as exports continued to outpace imports.

item.title: Sid Krofft, 96, Dies; Created Zany TV Shows for Children of All Ages
item.description: With his brother Marty, he released a string of wildly inventive programs, including “H.R. Pufnstuf” and “Land of the Lost.” Some became cult favorites, even among adults.
item.dc:creator: Daniel E. Slotnik
item.category: Sigmund and the Sea Monsters (TV Program)
item.media:description: Sid Krofft in 2009 accepting an honor at the Seventh Annual TV Land Awards ceremony in Los Angeles. With him was a version of his creation H.R. Pufnstuf.

item.title: A Famed Brooklyn Cemetery Wants You to Come Visit … Before You Die
item.description: Green-Wood Cemetery in Brooklyn is opening a $43 million visitor’s center to attract the living by making it easier to navigate the rambling grounds.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu
item.media:description: Green-Wood Cemetery’s new visitor’s center has been built around a restored Victorian greenhouse.

item.title: I Went to China to See Their Progress on A.I. We Can’t Beat Them.
item.description: American leaders should be trying to cooperate with China on A.I.
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Mallaby

item.title: Is It Too Much to Ask to Care About Sudan?
item.description: I have seen what can happen when Americans care about a wrong in the world.
item.dc:creator: Ann Curry
item.category: United Nations High Commission for Refugees

item.title: Late Night Takes Sides in the President vs. the Pope
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel said President Trump’s social media post aimed at Pope Leo XIV “is what happens when you sell Bibles instead of reading them.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Oasis, Phil Collins and Sade to Join Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Billy Idol, Luther Vandross and Wu-Tang Clan will also be inducted, while New Edition, Mariah Carey and Melissa Etheridge failed to make the final cut.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.category: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame and Museum

item.title: About 90 People Detained at N.Y.C. Protest Over Arms Sales to Israel
item.description: The protesters sat in the middle of Third Avenue in Midtown Manhattan and blocked traffic for about an hour.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman, Olivia Bensimon and Wesley Parnell
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Netflix Leader Makes Rare Overture to Cinema Owners
item.description: Ted Sarandos attended a major movie theater conference for the first time and met with domestic and international owners, people familiar with the meetings said.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ted Sarandos, Netflix’s co-chief executive, in January. He held two separate meetings on Sunday with top domestic and international theater owners, four people said.
